steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: ”Hidden but still conscious glory suited the nature of the man”—combined with his love of sitting silently, thinking how politicians are “in his power.” This shows he derives satisfaction from private, unacknowledged influence.

steps.title: Match D to the passage
steps.content:
D is correct: Towers takes private satisfaction in his capacity to influence public opinion.
The others fail: A claims people focus on him (they don't), B invents praise and overestimation without support, and C suggests he seeks far-away readers' admiration (contradicted—he's satisfied with being unknown).
That matches choice D — D is correct.
